task: Fix discovered OAuth2 MCP re-auth loop by treating invalid_grant ReAuthenticationRequired like TokenRefreshError in resolver.py (invalidate + return False), with optional Approach C safety net in _initiate.py to run discovery when no snapshot exists.

key_reasoning.reason: Approach A is a single method change in MCPAuthResolver._resolve_discovered_server (resolver.py, codemie-enterprise); Approach C adds one endpoint handler change in build_discovered_oauth2_initiate_response (_initiate.py, codemie). Two repos, two components, but both changes are narrow and self-contained within the MCP auth subsystem.

The fix is in OAuth2 authentication logic — a security-sensitive area. The change mirrors the existing TokenRefreshError handler verbatim (same helper, same return value), so implementation risk is low. One subtle risk: invalidate_if_stale semantics for an invalid_grant token must be confirmed as a hard permanent invalidation, not a transient-stale check. Auth red flag applied: bumped from S (2) to M (3).

red_flags_applied: Technical Risk bumped from S to M: change affects OAuth2 authentication/authorization logic (auth red flag)
